timeline.label: Announced pending acquisition expanding quantum networking stack (Skyloom closed per Q1 2026 10-Q)
Q1 2026 revenue $64.7M (+755% YoY) but operating loss ($271.5M); cash $493.5M after acquisitions
FY2025 revenue $130.0M (+202% YoY); net loss $512.1M; OCF ($283.2M); cash $1,030.9M
Closed Vector Atomic acquisition — atomic clocks and sensors for quantum stack
Closed Oxford Ionics acquisition — ion-trap chip technology
Closed Capella Space acquisition — SAR satellite imagery and data platform
Closed id Quantique SA acquisition — quantum networking and cryptography
Equity and warrant financings raised ~$3.4B net cash from financing activities in FY2025
NYSE listing via dMY Technology Group III SPAC merger — public float and warrant overhang
Company founded on trapped-ion quantum computing approach (University of Maryland / Duke lineage)

industry.structure: Fragmented early-stage quantum computing market with modality competition (trapped-ion, superconducting, photonic, neutral atom); heavy government and hyperscaler funding; few scaled revenue pools.
industry.share_shift: IonQ gaining reported revenue share via hardware contracts and acquisitive expansion into networking and sensing; IBM and Google remain research leaders with cloud access models.
industry.trend: Consolidation into full-stack quantum platforms; defense and national-security budgets increasing; error correction and networking seen as next bottlenecks after qubit count.
